source.title: BSEE Gulf of Mexico Region Accident Investigation Report - Shell Offshore Inc. MC 807

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Mississippi Canyon Block 807/943

actions_taken: ROV closed SCSSV control valves SV1 and SV2
Well shut in and pressure continuously monitored
Leak test performed to ensure integrity
Reported to National Response Center (NRC #1357058 and #1357318)
Verbally reported to BSEE New Orleans District
event.summary: On January 6, 2023, at 13:00 hours, Shell Offshore Inc. experienced a pollution event at Mississippi Canyon 943 subsea well PN003-ST2-BP1, which is tied back to MC 807 B (Olympus). While attempting to open the Surface-Controlled Subsurface Safety Valve (SCSSV), there was an inability to build pressure in the SCSSV control lines and abnormal pressure behavior at the tubing and A-annulus head. An underwater ROV confirmed liquid being released to the environment through the High Pressure vent on the tree due to SCSSV control lines loss of integrity. The fluid mixture consisted of hydraulic fluid, methanol, calcium bromide completion brine, and synthetic based oil. Initial estimate was 9.26 gallons, later updated to 16.61 barrels total. The ROV closed control valves SV1 and SV2, stopping the leak. The well remains shut in with 2 barriers and pressure is being continuously monitored. The tubing is suspected to be parted at a shallow depth along with the SCSSV control lines, allowing a leak path from the wellbore to the SCSSV control lines to the HP vent.
recommendations: Shell to perform intervention to remove tubing for analysis
Shell to identify causes of failure after tubing recovery
BSEE to engage with Shell's analysis to inform policy and procedure to prevent future reoccurrence
key_phrases: SCSSV control lines loss of integrity
tubing suspected to be parted at shallow depth
leak path from wellbore to SCSSV control lines to HP vent
inability to build pressure in SCSSV control lines
abnormal pressure behavior at tubing and A-annulus head

hazards.name: Pressurized hydrocarbons and completion fluids in subsea well

threats.name: SCSSV control line loss of integrity

consequences.name: Environmental pollution - release of fluids to sea

controls.name: Surface-Controlled Subsurface Safety Valve (SCSSV)
controls.lod_basis: Primary downhole barrier to prevent flow

supporting_text: in attempting to open the Surface-Controlled Subsurface Safety Valve (SCSSV), there was an inability to build pressure in the SCSSV control lines
the pressure loss was from liquid being released to the environment through the High Pressure (HP) vent on the tree due to the SCSSV control lines loss of integrity
The tubing is suspected to be parted at a shallow depth along with the SCSSV control lines, allowing a leak path from the wellbore to the SCSSV control lines to the HP vent

controls.name: Tubing integrity
controls.barrier_role: Contain wellbore fluids within tubing string

supporting_text: The tubing is suspected to be parted at a shallow depth
abnormal pressure behavior at the tubing and A-annulus head
The tubing will be pulled, and new tubing will be re-run before bringing the well back online

controls.name: Subsea tree control valves (SV1 and SV2)
controls.barrier_role: Isolate SCSSV control lines from HP vent
controls.lod_basis: Secondary isolation capability to stop leak path

supporting_text: the ROV closed the SCSSV control valves SV1 and SV2. Then visually confirmed the leak had stopped
The SV1 and SV2 needle valves remain closed on the PN003 Tree to isolate leak path to environment and performed a leak test to ensure integrity

The well was restored to a 2-barrier status that was conducted on January 6, 2023, by closing and pressure testing SV1 and SV2 needle valves (isolating the leak path through the HP vent on the subsea tree)

controls.name: Remote Operated Vehicle (ROV) monitoring and intervention
controls.barrier_role: Detect leak and enable manual intervention

supporting_text: The environmental release was observed by an underwater Remote Operated Vehicle (ROV) during diagnostics on the PN003 subsea tree
the ROV closed the SCSSV control valves SV1 and SV2. Then visually confirmed the leak had stopped

controls.name: FS2 fluid loss valve
controls.barrier_role: Provide barrier to prevent fluid loss

supporting_text: The well is currently shut in and secure with 2 barriers, FS2 (fluid loss valve) and subsea tree
The well is currently shut in and secure with 2 barriers, FS2 (fluid loss valve) and subsea tree
The well remains shut in and the pressure is being continuously monitored

controls.name: Continuous pressure monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Detect abnormal conditions and verify barrier integrity

supporting_text: abnormal pressure behavior at the tubing and A-annulus head
Pressure at the tubing/A-annulus head is being continuously monitored
The pressure remains stable above seawater hydrostatic and below pressure to balance reservoir pressure, indicating both barriers have integrity

controls.name: National Response Center (NRC) reporting procedure
controls.barrier_role: Ensure regulatory notification and emergency response coordination

supporting_text: The release was reported to the National Response Center (NRC) and verbally to the Bureau of Safety and Environmental Enforcement (BSEE) New Orleans District (NOD)
There was a report submitted to the National Response Center (NRC) #1357058 on January 7, 2023
On January 10, 2023, another NRC report was submitted. NRC report #1357318 was updated to include a total of 16.61 total barrels

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
